name: domain-fusion-iter
description: Use web_search to search for 'ITER project status update timeline delay cost 2025 2026'. Results will overlap with earlier fusion searches. What is the current status of ITER? Summarize the timeline, cost overruns, and expected first plasma date. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: ITER, the 35-nation international tokamak project in southern France, has experienced repeated delays and massive cost overruns. Originally projected for completion by 2016 at ~$10 billion, the timeline has been pushed back multiple times. Under the 2024 revised baseline, first plasma (deuterium-only) is now scheduled for 2034, full plasma current by 2036, and deuterium-tritium operations by 2039. Total costs have escalated from an initial €6 billion to an estimated €18-22 billion, with an additional €5 billion announced in 2024. Delays stem from manufacturing faults, COVID-19, management complexity of multi-nation contributions, and the decision to switch first-wall material from beryllium to tungsten. Despite setbacks, ITER reports strong recent progress on assembly and all superconducting magnets are now complete.
key_facts: ITER first plasma now scheduled for 2034, D-T operations by 2039
Total costs estimated at €18-22 billion, up from original €6 billion
An additional €5 billion cost increase was announced in July 2024
35 nations participate in the project, originally projected for 2016 completion
All superconducting magnets have been completed and tokamak assembly is progressing
